equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: Ritual add-ons retain a scheduler address but explicitly delegate scheduling initialization elsewhere and do not create or spawn ScheduledTxService.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing creation of ScheduledTxService with node provider, pool, and configured scheduler address, and missing task executor spawn of service.start().
related_but_insufficient_code: ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal/crates/node/src/addons.rs reads/logs scheduler_address but comments that scheduler initialization is handled by another binary.
launch_add_ons handles optional debug consensus and then delegates to self.inner.launch_add_ons(ctx) without constructing or spawning a ScheduledTxService.
No ScheduledTxService symbol exists in the audited nodebuilder, port, or ritual-node-internal checkouts; ScheduledTxService matches only the non-audited legacy checkout.

rationale: launch_add_ons now obtains provider, pool, and task executor, constructs ScheduledTxService::new(provider, pool, scheduler_address), and spawns scheduled_tx_service.start(). The service constructor was simplified to those three runtime dependencies. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When Ritual add-ons launch, they must create ScheduledTxService with the node provider, transaction pool, and configured Scheduler address, then spawn it on the node task executor.
suggested_falsification_tests: Launch node add-ons with a test task executor and pool/provider mocks, and assert ScheduledTxService::start is spawned with the configured Scheduler address.
summary: Ritual add-ons do not start the scheduling service in the audited repo set.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Launch RitualAddOns with a recording task executor; current audited nodebuilder code should not spawn ScheduledTxService::start.
title: Add-ons start scheduling service
